response:
The hexagonal community center possesses an area derived from six equilateral triangles each with side length thirty meters yielding a total surface of nine hundred root three square meters. This geometric calculation relies on the standard formula for regular polygons where the side length determines the altitude of constituent triangles through trigonometric sine functions.

The circular garden inscribed within the hexagon has a radius equal to the apothem of the polygon which measures fifteen root three meters resulting in a garden area of six hundred seventy-five pi square meters. This radius represents the perpendicular distance from the center to the midpoint of any side ensuring the circumference touches all six boundaries exactly as planned.
